name: PUBLIC_BASE_URL override for self-hosted upload URLs
description: When the backend sits behind a TLS terminator that does not forward X-Forwarded-Proto (notably `tailscale serve`), the upload URLs returned by GET /v1.4/:dongle_id/upload_url/ and embedded in uploadFilesToUrls RPCs fall back to http://, so the device PUTs to a port that is not listening and gets a 404. Add an operator-controlled PUBLIC_BASE_URL config that, when set, overrides scheme+host derivation for every device-facing upload URL the backend mints.
acceptance_criteria: Config loads PUBLIC_BASE_URL from the environment; empty preserves the existing schemeFromRequest behaviour
PUBLIC_BASE_URL is validated at startup: must start with http:// or https://, must include a host, must not end in a trailing slash
When set, GET /v1.4/:dongle_id/upload_url/ returns URLs prefixed with PUBLIC_BASE_URL regardless of incoming Host or X-Forwarded-Proto headers (covers both segment and boot paths)
When set, RouteDataRequestHandler.buildUploadItems uses PUBLIC_BASE_URL as the baseURL for every uploadFilesToUrls payload
Tests cover: PUBLIC_BASE_URL set wins over X-Forwarded-Proto, PUBLIC_BASE_URL empty preserves existing fallback, config validation rejects malformed values
go build, go vet, gofmt all pass; smoke.sh passes
